- [ ] Verify that the copilot-instructions.md file in the .github directory is created.
- [ ] Clarify Project Requirements
- [ ] Scaffold the Project
- [ ] Customize the Project
- [ ] Install Required Extensions
- [ ] Compile the Project
- [ ] Create and Run Task
- [ ] Launch the Project
- [ ] Ensure Documentation is Complete

Execution Guidelines
- Progress tracking: use available todo tooling; mark each step after completion; read status before starting a new step.
- Communication: stay concise; avoid full command outputs; note skipped steps briefly.
- Development: use '.' as the working directory; avoid media/links unless required; mark placeholders clearly; use the VS Code API tool only for VS Code extensions; the project is already open.
- Folder creation: use the current directory; use '.' in commands; do not create new folders unless requested, aside from .vscode/tasks.json when needed.
- Extension installation: install only extensions specified by get_project_setup_info.
- Project content: assume "Hello World" if unspecified; avoid unnecessary links or integrations; avoid media unless requested; label placeholders; confirm features when unsure; use VS Code API tool for VS Code extension work.
- Task completion: project scaffolded and compiled; copilot-instructions.md exists; README.md is current; user has instructions to debug/launch the project.
- Work through each checklist item systematically; keep communication concise; follow development best practices.
